emotions.name: Ominous
emotions.description: dread creeping at the edges

emotions.name: Reverent
emotions.description: sacred, hushed, numinous

emotions.name: Melancholic
emotions.description: grief-touched, sorrowful

emotions.name: Triumphant
emotions.description: victory, exultation

emotions.name: Eerie
emotions.description: wrongness, the uncanny

emotions.name: Wistful
emotions.description: nostalgic longing

emotions.name: Grim
emotions.description: bleak resolve, gallows determination

emotions.name: Wonder
emotions.description: awe at the sublime or magical

emotions.name: Sinister
emotions.description: malice lurking beneath

emotions.name: Elegiac
emotions.description: mourning what's been lost

emotions.name: Desperate
emotions.description: backs against the wall

emotions.name: Tender
emotions.description: quiet intimacy, vulnerability

emotions.name: Defiant
emotions.description: fury against the inevitable

emotions.name: Solemn
emotions.description: weight of ceremony or oath

emotions.name: Frenzied
emotions.description: chaos, madness, battle-fury

emotions.name: Desolate
emotions.description: emptiness, ruin, aftermath

emotions.name: Sardonic
emotions.description: bitter humor, gallows wit

emotions.name: Paranoid
emotions.description: suspicion, walls closing in

emotions.name: Rapturous
emotions.description: ecstasy, transcendence

emotions.name: Brooding
emotions.description: dark introspection, storm-before-the-storm

emotions.name: Awe
emotions.description: Vastness that makes characters feel small—cathedrals, dragons, ancient magic, star-thick skies.

emotions.name: Dread
emotions.description: A slow, tightening sense that something is wrong, unseen, and closing in.

emotions.name: Wonder
emotions.description: Curiosity-lit delight at the strange and beautiful, like discovering a hidden grove or impossible artifact.

emotions.name: Foreboding
emotions.description: The scene feels like a dark prologue—omens, uneasy silences, and the sense of an approaching turn.

emotions.name: Triumph
emotions.description: A hard-won surge of victory, often edged by exhaustion, scars, or cost.

emotions.name: Reverence
emotions.description: A hushed, ceremonial respect—toward gods, relics, monarchs, vows, or the dead.

emotions.name: Melancholy
emotions.description: Soft grief without immediate crisis—ruins, fading songs, remembered homes, the end of an age.

emotions.name: Rage
emotions.description: Hot, propulsive fury—often righteous, often dangerous, narrowing the character’s world to a target.

emotions.name: Grief
emotions.description: Acute loss that bends everything in the scene toward absence, weight, and aftershock.

emotions.name: Tenderness
emotions.description: Brief warmth and care—bandaging wounds, shared food, small acts that resist the harsh world.

emotions.name: Suspicion
emotions.description: Motive-hunting alertness—every smile may be a knife; every gift may be bait.

emotions.name: Paranoia
emotions.description: Suspicion turned feverish—patterns everywhere, trust collapsing, safety feeling impossible.

emotions.name: Relief
emotions.description: Pressure releasing—survival after peril, a door barred, a friend found, a spell finally holds.

emotions.name: Hope
emotions.description: A thin but stubborn thread—belief that the quest, the cure, or the dawn is still possible.

emotions.name: Desperation
emotions.description: When choices shrink to ugly options—bargains, sacrifices, last spells, last arrows.

emotions.name: Elation
emotions.description: Bright, almost reckless joy—flight, feast, reunion, magic working cleanly for once.

emotions.name: Humiliation
emotions.description: Status and pride torn open—public failure, betrayal, being outplayed in court or combat.

emotions.name: Stoic Resolve
emotions.description: A controlled, iron steadiness—fear acknowledged but harnessed; duty becomes the spine of the scene.

emotions.name: Mirth
emotions.description: Levity in the mud—banter, gallows humor, tavern laughter that keeps the dark at bay.

emotions.name: Uncanny Unease
emotions.description: Something is familiar but wrong—time slips, too-smooth voices, perfect rooms, magic that feels like teeth.

emotions.name: Awe
emotions.description: A stunned, widening sense of scale—something vast or holy makes the characters feel small and breathless.

emotions.name: Dread
emotions.description: A cold certainty that something is wrong, approaching, or already watching; fear without a clear shape.

emotions.name: Wonder
emotions.description: Curiosity laced with delight at the impossible made real—magic felt as discovery rather than threat.

emotions.name: Foreboding
emotions.description: A quiet, creeping suspicion that today’s choices will have consequences the characters can’t yet name.

emotions.name: Reverence
emotions.description: Respect edged with fear, as if speaking too loudly might offend a god, a relic, or an ancient law.

emotions.name: Triumph
emotions.description: A surge of victory—earned, costly, and bright—often with a lingering taste of what it took to win.

emotions.name: Grief
emotions.description: Loss that settles like ash; the world feels dimmer, and even the scenery seems to mourn.

emotions.name: Rage
emotions.description: Hot, focused fury that narrows the scene to a single purpose: punish, break, burn, or undo.

emotions.name: Resolve
emotions.description: A hardened calm—the moment a character decides to endure, to act, or to pay the price willingly.

emotions.name: Suspicion
emotions.description: Every word feels like a trap; motives are questioned, alliances feel thin, and trust becomes currency.

emotions.name: Paranoia
emotions.description: Suspicion turned inward and feverish—shadows become evidence, and coincidence feels like design.

emotions.name: Hope
emotions.description: A fragile light held against darkness—small, stubborn optimism that refuses to die.

emotions.name: Desperation
emotions.description: Running out of time, options, or breath; choices become ugly because survival is louder than pride.

emotions.name: Melancholy
emotions.description: A sweet ache for what’s gone or fading—ruins, old songs, old empires, old selves.

emotions.name: Nostalgia
emotions.description: Longing for a remembered place or simpler world, often sharpened by the knowledge it can’t be returned to.

emotions.name: Elation
emotions.description: A buoyant lift—relief and joy spilling over after danger passes or a miracle occurs.

emotions.name: Unease
emotions.description: Low-grade discomfort; nothing is overtly wrong, but the scene feels tilted, like a painting hung crooked.

emotions.name: Solemnity
emotions.description: Quiet gravity—oaths, funerals, crowns, prophecies—where even small gestures carry weight.

emotions.name: Enchantment
emotions.description: A dreamy, bewitched softness; the world feels more vivid, seductive, and slightly unreal.

emotions.name: Defiance
emotions.description: A sharp refusal to yield—standing against fate, tyrants, gods, or the story’s “inevitable” outcome.

emotions.name: Awe
emotions.description: A breath-held sense of vastness—ancient ruins, impossible skies, or power that makes characters feel small.

emotions.name: Dread
emotions.description: A slow, sinking certainty that something is wrong, approaching, or already inside the walls.

emotions.name: Wonder
emotions.description: Bright curiosity and delighted disbelief at the strange: new magic, unfamiliar creatures, or hidden worlds.

emotions.name: Foreboding
emotions.description: A quiet warning tone—omens, uneasy coincidences, and the feeling the story is turning toward trouble.

emotions.name: Reverence
emotions.description: A hushed, ritual respect for gods, kings, relics, or sacred places where even speech feels intrusive.

emotions.name: Defiance
emotions.description: Steel-spined refusal to yield—characters choose “no” even when the world insists otherwise.

emotions.name: Grief
emotions.description: Loss made heavy and tangible: a death, a burned home, a broken oath, or an age ending.

emotions.name: Rage
emotions.description: Hot, narrowing fury that sharpens decisions and risks turning justice into violence.

emotions.name: Hope
emotions.description: A fragile light—small victories, unlikely allies, or one promise that keeps the night from winning.

emotions.name: Desperation
emotions.description: The feeling of running out of road: last arrows, last spell, last chance to save someone.

emotions.name: Triumph
emotions.description: A surge of earned victory—after ordeal, the world briefly aligns and the hero can breathe.

emotions.name: Betrayal
emotions.description: A cold snap in trust—an ally’s turn, a revealed lie, or loyalty sold for gold or fear.

emotions.name: Suspicion
emotions.description: Watchful uncertainty—glances linger, motives feel layered, and every kindness might be a hook.

emotions.name: Tenderness
emotions.description: Softness amid hardship—care, intimacy, or protection that feels precious because it’s rare.

emotions.name: Melancholy
emotions.description: A lingering sadness without a single cause—beautiful decay, fading glory, and roads that don’t return.

emotions.name: Enchantment
emotions.description: A dreamlike, spell-touched mood where logic loosens and everything feels slightly luminous or unreal.

emotions.name: Unease
emotions.description: A prickling discomfort—too-quiet forests, polite smiles that don’t reach the eyes, wrong-footed reality.

emotions.name: Exaltation
emotions.description: A soaring, near-religious high—prophecy fulfilled, power awakened, or destiny suddenly close enough to touch.

emotions.name: Isolation
emotions.description: A sense of being cut off—geographically, socially, or spiritually—like shouting into a vast, indifferent world.

emotions.name: Determination
emotions.description: Steady forward motion—resolve that doesn’t need anger or hope, just a decision that won’t be undone.

emotions.name: Dread
emotions.description: Creeping anticipation of something terrible approaching

emotions.name: Wonder
emotions.description: Awe at encountering the vast, magical, or incomprehensible

emotions.name: Melancholy
emotions.description: A lingering sadness, often tied to loss or fading glory

emotions.name: Fury
emotions.description: Raw, explosive anger—battle rage or righteous wrath

emotions.name: Reverence
emotions.description: Humbled respect before something ancient or sacred

emotions.name: Despair
emotions.description: Utter hopelessness when all seems lost

emotions.name: Tension
emotions.description: Taut, electric anticipation before conflict or revelation

emotions.name: Triumph
emotions.description: Exultant victory, the payoff of struggle

emotions.name: Grief
emotions.description: Deep mourning for the fallen or the broken

emotions.name: Unease
emotions.description: Something is wrong but unnamed—wrongness in the air

emotions.name: Longing
emotions.description: Ache for something distant—home, love, a lost age

emotions.name: Terror
emotions.description: Visceral, primal fear in the face of true horror

emotions.name: Serenity
emotions.description: Stillness and peace, often before the storm

emotions.name: Defiance
emotions.description: Standing firm against overwhelming odds

emotions.name: Betrayal
emotions.description: The sting of broken trust, loyalty turned to ash

emotions.name: Mystery
emotions.description: The pull of secrets, riddles, and hidden knowledge

emotions.name: Intimacy
emotions.description: Closeness between characters—vulnerability shared

emotions.name: Foreboding
emotions.description: Dark omens and the shadow of things to come

emotions.name: Elation
emotions.description: Soaring joy, often fleeting in dark tales

emotions.name: Resolve
emotions.description: Grim determination hardening into purpose

emotions.name: Dread
emotions.description: Creeping awareness of impending doom; the weight of something terrible approaching.

emotions.name: Wonder
emotions.description: Breathless awe at the magical, vast, or sublime.

emotions.name: Melancholy
emotions.description: Deep, lingering sadness often tied to loss, decay, or fading glory.

emotions.name: Triumph
emotions.description: Victorious exultation; the surge of hard-won success.

emotions.name: Foreboding
emotions.description: Ominous anticipation; dark clouds gathering on the horizon.

emotions.name: Reverence
emotions.description: Sacred awe; the hush before something holy or ancient.

emotions.name: Desperation
emotions.description: Frantic urgency when all options narrow to one last gamble.

emotions.name: Tension
emotions.description: Taut anticipation; a bowstring drawn and waiting to snap.

emotions.name: Grief
emotions.description: Raw, immediate mourning; the wound of fresh loss.

emotions.name: Menace
emotions.description: Threatening atmosphere radiating from a presence or place.

emotions.name: Desolation
emotions.description: Bleak emptiness; the silence of ruins and abandoned things.

emotions.name: Intimacy
emotions.description: Close personal connection; vulnerability shared between souls.

emotions.name: Fury
emotions.description: Burning, barely-contained rage seeking an outlet.

emotions.name: Serenity
emotions.description: Peaceful stillness; a moment of calm amid chaos.

emotions.name: Horror
emotions.description: Visceral revulsion and fear at something fundamentally wrong.

emotions.name: Resolve
emotions.description: Steely determination; the quiet hardening before action.

emotions.name: Nostalgia
emotions.description: Bittersweet longing for a past that cannot return.

emotions.name: Unease
emotions.description: Subtle wrongness; something off that can't quite be named.

emotions.name: Exhilaration
emotions.description: Thrilling rush of danger, speed, or sudden freedom.

emotions.name: Solemnity
emotions.description: Grave seriousness; the weight of oaths, rituals, or final words.
